item.title: Govt launches nationwide anti-dengue drive
item.description: The government on Saturday launched a three-month nationwide campaign to raise public awareness about dengue prevention, eliminate breeding sites of aedes mosquitoes and ensure a clean environment....

item.title: Easy-bike driver arrested for ‘raping’ disabled minor girl in Narail
item.description: The police on Saturday arrested an easy-bike driver on charges of abducting and raping a 13-year-old girl with disabilities at Lohagara in Narail....

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 50 victims, arrests 9 traffickers at Teknaf
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard rescued 50 victims from a Malaysia-bound boat and arrested 9 human traffickers conducting an operation in the sea area adjacent to Baharchara under Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on Friday night....

item.title: Navy arrests 52 while smuggling cement to Myanmar
item.description: Bangladesh Navy arrested 1,450 bags of cement, five engine-powered wooden fishing boats and 52 members of a smuggling gang during a patrol operation in the Bay of Bengal on Thursday night....

item.title: Bangla Academy organises commemorative seminar series
item.description: Bangla Academy organised a commemorative seminar series on Saturday in memory of poet Jibanananda Das, poet Al Mahmud, writer and translator Moniruddin Yusuf and fiction writer Rashid Karim at its Kabi Shamsur Rahman seminar room....

item.title: Islami Bank, Janata hold 30pc of total NPLs
item.description: Only two banks — Islami Bank Bangladesh and Janata Bank — accounted for Tk 1,70,625 crore in classified loans by March 2026, representing nearly 30 per cent of the banking sector’s total defaulted loans, according to Bangladesh Bank data......

item.title: Leather sector blames incomplete CETP for export stagnation
item.description: Bangladesh’s leather industry leaders blamed prolonged delays in establishing a fully functional central effluent treatment plant and inconsistent government policies for the sector’s stagnant exports despite the country’s abundant supply of raw hides......

item.title: 20th denim expo starts June 10
item.description: The two-day 20th edition of the Bangladesh Denim Expo would begin in Dhaka on June 10, bringing together global industry stakeholders to discuss the future of Bangladesh's denim and apparel sector amid evolving economic and trade challenges......

item.title: RMG exports to major markets fall in July-May
item.description: The country’s readymade garment exports to major global destinations declined persistently during the July-May period of the current financial year 2025-26 due to weak global demand......

item.title: New BSCIC industrial estate to be built in Pabna
item.description: Commerce, textiles and jute, and industries minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir has said a new BSCIC industrial estate will be established in Pabna district within the shortest possible time......

item.title: Tk 25,000cr fund sought for renewable energy
item.description: Civil society organisations and energy experts on Saturday urged the government to introduce a symbolic 1 per cent tax on renewable energy equipment in the upcoming national budget and establish a Tk 25,000 crore revolving green finance facility to accelerate the country’s transition to clean energy and strengthen energy security......

item.title: Strong US job growth beats expectations in May
item.description: US job growth surged past expectations in May with the unemployment rate remaining steady, as the labor market in the world’s largest economy showed signs of firming after months of turbulence......

item.title: MEASLES: High treatment costs hit families hard
item.description: The escalating measles outbreak in Bangladesh is not only emotionally traumatising families, but also putting them in severe hardship due to mounting hospital bills....

item.title: LDC transition period extension opportunity for Bangladesh
item.description: The World Trade Organization has underscored the importance of utilizing any extended transition period for Least Developed Country graduation to strengthen productive capacity, improve competitiveness, diversify exports and prepare for operating under standard WTO rules......

item.title: Dinajpur’s traditional papad eyes global market
item.description: The century-old tradition of producing Dinajpur’s famous papad or papor, once renowned across the Indian subcontinent for its unique taste and quality, now requires government support to regain its lost glory and expand into international markets......

item.title: Airliners gather in Rio to chart course as horizon darkens
item.description: Airline executives descend on Rio this weekend to weigh the prospects for an industry grappling with geopolitical turbulence, soaring fuel costs and travellers wary of chasing sky-high ticket prices......

item.title: SpaceX signs pre-IPO deal to provide AI computing to Google
item.description: SpaceX on Friday signed a blockbuster cloud computing agreement under which Google will pay the Elon Musk-founded rocket company $920 million per month for access to a massive cluster of AI chips, according to a disclosure in its initial public offering filing......

item.title: Indonesian hotel chain confirms exit from Cuba
item.description: Indonesian hotel chain Archipelago International has confirmed its exit from Cuba citing US sanctions, with the group telling AFP on Friday it may resume operations ‘if the situation improves’......

item.title: Video game makers shift releases to avoid ‘GTA VI’ juggernaut
item.description: Video game publishers are reshuffling release schedules to avoid getting run over by feverishly anticipated ‘Grand Theft Auto VI’, as a crowded autumn line-up takes shape, industry players said at an annual gaming event Friday......

item.title: New Yorkers wait in line for trendy foods
item.description: In front of L’industrie Pizzeria, as at many other New York restaurants and shops, customers line up behind barriers—in this case, all to grab a prized pizza slice......

item.title: Nvidia’s Huang arrives in S Korea, bets on robotics
item.description: Nvidia chief executive Jensen Huang arrived in Seoul Friday for a packed schedule of meetings with tech leaders, promising ‘some surprises’ for South Korea while predicting robotics will be the country’s next major growth sector......

item.title: Canada sees surprise jobs hike
item.description: Canada’s unemployment rate dropped to 6.6 percent in May on an unexpected spike in hirings across multiple sectors, partially offsetting job losses earlier in the year, government data showed Friday......

item.title: Tech sell-off, rate-hike fears drive Wall Street plunge
item.description: Wall Street’s key indices closed heavily in the red on Friday, hit by a massive sell-off in technology stocks following a recent surge driven by AI investment, and fears of US Fed rate hikes on the horizon......

item.title: Israeli strike kills three Lebanese soldiers
item.description: An Israeli strike on a vehicle in southern Lebanon killed three soldiers, Beirut’s military said Saturday, days after the countries agreed a conditional truce during talks in the United States....

item.title: Deep-sea Miner TMC sues international regulator
item.description: The Metals Company (TMC), a Canadian firm focused on deep-sea mining on the high seas, has taken its dispute with the global regulatory body overseeing the nascent and controversial industry to an international tribunal......

item.title: Iran targets Bahrain, Kuwait after US strikes
item.description: Iran launched fresh aerial attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait early Saturday, after the United States said it had struck the Islamic republic....

item.title: Agrani Bank inaugurates foundation course
item.description: Agrani Bank Training Institute inaugurated a 30-day foundation course for the 98th and 99th batches of cash officers on Saturday......

item.title: PM invites Turkish president to visit Bangladesh
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Saturday extended an invitation to Turkish president Recep Tayyip Erdogan to visit Bangladesh at a mutually convenient time....

item.title: Karmasangsthan Bank holds business review meeting in Sylhet
item.description: Karmasngsthan Bank organised business review meeting-2026 on Saturday as part of the 90-day special programme at Rainbow Guest House in Sylhet with participation from all regional managers, branch managers, and second officers......

item.title: BRAC Bank Reading Cafés host discussion on Dipu Mahmud’s ‘Breakup'
item.description: Bangladeshi author Dipu Mahmud engaged with readers at separate sessions hosted by BRAC Bank Sepal Reading Café and BRAC Bank Chuadanga Reading Café, where his short story collection Breakup was discussed in depth......

item.title: Daraz launches 6.6 mid-year shopping fest with discounts
item.description: Daraz Bangladesh on Friday launched its much-anticipated 6.6 Mid-Year Shopping Fest, offering customers across the country an exciting opportunity to enjoy exceptional savings, exclusive brand offers, and rewarding shopping experiences......

item.title: MetLife installs solar panel at head office
item.description: MetLife Bangladesh has installed a 104-kilowatt on-grid solar panel system at its Motijheel head office, enabling the company to generate up to 10 per cent of its electricity needs from solar power......

item.title: Seven journalists honoured with Agro-Industry award
Pallabi child rape, murder verdict today
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Women and Children Repression Prevention Tribunal is set to deliver its verdict today in the case over the rape and murder of an eight-year-old girl in the capital’s Pallabi area on May 19....

item.title: New Age passes 23 years of outspoken journalism
item.description: NEW Age steps into its 24th year today. For the 23 years that the newspaper completed the day before, we have held the fort against any odds that have surfaced to practise the kind of journalism that is independent, distinct, credible and committed to the tenets that hold power to account, inform the readers of what goes on inside the political and business quarters and......

item.title: Due process must prevail over push-in tactics
item.description: THE recent spate of India’s illegal cross-border push-in attempts, despite Dhaka’s assurances of cooperation through due process, appears to be Delhi’s blatant disregard for the international law, bilateral agreements and the good neighbourly spirit. While India has continued such unneighbourly behaviour since the latter half of 2024 and pushed at least 2,463......

item.title: Cost of youth unemployment
item.description: BANGLADESH has tended to be optimistic about its demographic dividend for good reasons. More than a half the population is under 35 and each year thousands of young people join the job market with the dreams of their families that have sacrificed, saved and educated them. However, the concern of increasing economic and social costs of youth unemployment......

item.title: From talk to action
item.description: BANGLADESH today stands at the frontline of a deepening climate crisis. Rapid urbanisation, industrial expansion and demographic pressure converge with intensifying climate shocks to create a complex and interconnected challenge. Food security, agriculture and livestock, employment and environmental stability are no longer separate concerns; they are interwoven......

item.title: No scope for return of shrine crocodile
item.description: ON THE afternoon of June 3, the Bangladesh Forest Department, the Khulna Wildlife Rescue and Rehabilitation Centre, the Karamjal Wildlife Breeding Centre and local administration captured and relocated a 45-year-old, 600-kilogram female marsh crocodile (Crocodylus palustris) from the Thakur Dighi adjacent to the Hazrat Khan Jahan Ali shrine in Bagerhat. The......

item.title: End of road for Starmer and Britain’s crisis of meaning
item.description: THE recent local elections in the United Kingdom revealed something deeper than a difficult electoral cycle for a governing party. They exposed a broader crisis of political legitimacy in a state whose traditional party system, territorial cohesion and governing narratives are all weakening simultaneously. Labour’s heavy losses across England, the continuing erosion of......

item.title: 37 AL activists arrested over processions in Noakhali, Ctg
item.description: The police arrested 37 leaders and activists of the Awami League, whose activities are now banned, and its banned student wing Bangladesh Chhatra league and other affiliated bodies in Noakhali and Chattogram on Saturday over the allegations of......

item.title: Five BNP men sued over alleged rape, looting in Rajshahi
item.description: A case was filed against five leaders and activists of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party and its associate organisations on charges of rape and looting of a woman at Puthia upazila in Rajshahi....

item.title: 8 transport operators fined for overcharging passengers in Sirajganj
item.description: A mobile court in Sirajganj has fined eight transport operators Tk 89,000 for allegedly charging passengers excessive fares during the return journey at the end of Eid holiday....

item.title: Research policy for pry edn ministry officials, teachers published
item.description: A policy for the officials and teachers under the primary and mass education ministry has been published recently to help them improve professionalism and the quality of primary and non-formal education....

item.title: Info minister calls for action plan to tackle fake news, digital risks
item.description: Information and Broadcasting Minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Saturday called on experts to submit a draft national action plan to address fake news, misinformation, disinformation and growing digital risks, stressing that the time had come for practical solutions...

item.title: BNP activists involved in many mob incidents, says NCP
item.description: National Citizen Party spokesperson Asif Mahmud Shojib Bhuiyan on Saturday alleged that individuals linked with the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party were involved in many mob-related incidents across the country....

item.title: Ukraine hits Russia with drones at forum’s close
item.description: Ukraine fired hundreds of drones at Russia early Saturday, leaving one person dead and setting an oil depot ablaze on the final day of the country’s flagship economic forum in Saint Petersburg, officials said....

item.title: Probe body formed over HSIA cargo village fire
item.description: A probe body has been formed to investigate the fire that broke out at the cargo village of Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka on Friday night, which was brought under control shortly....

item.title: Historic Six-Point Day today
item.description: Today is historic Six-Point Day, commemorating the movement and sacrifices made in the struggle to realise the demand for autonomy for the then East Pakistan, now Bangladesh....

item.title: City corporation worker shot at Jatrabari
item.description: An employee of the Dhaka South City Corporation was shot by miscreants in the capital’s Jatrabari area on Friday night....

item.title: BGB member found hanging at Chuadanga barracks
item.description: THE police recovered the hanging body of a member of the Border Guard Bangladesh from a room inside the barracks in Chuadanga on Saturday....

item.title: Wet spell may continue as monsoon advances
item.description: The ongoing wet spell is likely to persist through the weekend with monsoon setting in....

item.title: Health experts for separate budget allocation to control hypertension
item.description: Public health experts, policymakers, researchers and development partners have called for separate budget allocation, strengthening of the drug supply system and preventive activities through primary healthcare to ensure uninterrupted......

item.title: Meeting on assessment of cyclotron-based PET radiopharma held
item.description: A strategic meeting titled Demand Assessment of Cyclotron-Based PET Radiopharmaceuticals in Private and Government PET-CT Centres and Strengthening the Supply System of Bangladesh Atomic Energy Commission was held at BAEC of Agargaon in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.title: World must compensate countries
item.description: Young Women for Development Rights and Climate and Nari Unnayan Shakti jointly held a rooftop gardening promotion and heat stroke awareness workshop, marking World Environment Day, at the training centre of NUS at Banasri in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.title: A Turning Moment explores prints beyond colonial narratives
item.description: A group print exhibition titled ‘A Turning Moment’, which explores artwork beyond colonial narratives, is currently under way....

item.description: BotTala Actors’ Studio premiered its children’s theatre production titled ‘Dakat Halum Chitpatang’ at the Bangladesh Mahila Samity on Saturday....
